edges.mechanism: Red light activates phytochrome B photoreceptor; Pfr form accumulates in light.

evidence.title: The sorghum photoperiod sensitivity gene, Ma3, encodes a phytochrome B
evidence.authors: Childs KL, Miller FR, Cordonnier-Pratt MM, Pratt LH, Morgan PW, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: ma3 sorghum lacks a 123-KD phytochrome that predominates in light-grown plants and that is present in non-ma3 plants.
evidence.claim: Light activates PhyB (Ma3) expression / Pfr accumulation

edges.mechanism: Far-red light activates phytochrome A photoreceptor. SbPhyA is one of three sorghum phytochromes on chr 1.

evidence.title: Association analysis of photoperiodic flowering time genes in west and central African sorghum
evidence.authors: Bhosale SU, Stich B, Rattunde HFW, Weltzien E, Haussmann BIG, Hash CT, Melchinger AE, Parzies HK

evidence.claim: Sorghum has three phytochromes (PhyA/B/C) acting as light photoreceptors in flowering pathway

edges.mechanism: Phytochrome C is activated by red light; Ma5 locus encodes SbPhyC.

evidence.title: Deleterious mutations predicted in the sorghum Maturity and Dwarf genes from whole-genome resequencing

evidence.evidence_sentence: Ma5 = SbPhyC (Sobic.001G087100, Chr01)
evidence.claim: Ma5 locus encodes Phytochrome C, a light photoreceptor

edges.mechanism: Cryptochrome 1 perceives blue light; DNA-photolyase domain required for activity.

evidence.title: Association analysis of photoperiodic flowering time genes in west and central African sorghum
evidence.authors: Bhosale SU, Stich B, Rattunde HFW, Weltzien E, Haussmann BIG, Hash CT, Melchinger AE, Parzies HK
evidence.evidence_sentence: CRY1-b1 SNP722...Located in DNA photolyase domain; affects blue light reception capacity.
evidence.claim: Cryptochrome 1 is a blue-light photoreceptor in sorghum flowering

edges.mechanism: SbFKF1/SbFFL contains a LOV domain that forms a covalent adduct with FMN upon blue-light illumination, activating the F-box protein to recruit substrates via SCF ubiquitin ligase.

evidence.title: A sorghum gigantea mutant attenuates florigen gene expression and delays flowering time
evidence.authors: Abdul-Awal SM, Chen J, Xin Z, Harmon FG
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbGI physically interacts with the sorghum FKF1-like (SbFFL) protein, and the SbGI-SbFFL interaction is stimulated by blue light.

edges.mechanism: PhyB (Ma3) is required for evening-light activation of SbPRR37 in long days; loss of PhyB eliminates the evening expression peak of SbPRR37.

evidence.title: Sorghum Phytochrome B Inhibits Flowering in Long Days by Activating Expression of SbPRR37 and SbGHD7
evidence.authors: Yang S, Weers BD, Morishige DT, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: PhyB is required for light activation of SbPRR37 and SbGHD7 expression in the evening of long days.

edges.mechanism: PhyB activates SbGhd7 expression through evening-phase light signaling in long days.

evidence.title: Sorghum Phytochrome B Inhibits Flowering in Long Days by Activating Expression of SbPRR37 and SbGHD7
evidence.authors: Yang S, Weers BD, Morishige DT, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: Sorghum phytochrome B inhibits flowering in long days by activating expression of SbPRR37 and SbGHD7, repressors of SbEHD1, SbCN8 and SbCN12.
evidence.claim: PhyB activates SbGhd7 expression in LD

edges.mechanism: PhyB represses SbCN15 (SbFT1 = Hd3a-subclade florigen) via a mechanism that is PRR37/Ghd7-independent and photoperiod-independent. Loss of PhyB causes ~60-fold upregulation of SbCN15.

evidence.title: Sorghum Phytochrome B Inhibits Flowering in Long Days by Activating Expression of SbPRR37 and SbGHD7
evidence.authors: Yang S, Weers BD, Morishige DT, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: PhyB mediated repression of SbCN15 expression occurs regardless of photoperiod.

edges.mechanism: Phytochrome C (Ma5) acts together with PhyB to delay flowering in LD through activation of floral repressors; PhyC-light signaling contributes to PRR37 activation.

evidence.title: A sorghum gigantea mutant attenuates florigen gene expression and delays flowering time

evidence.title: CONSTANS is a photoperiod regulated activator of flowering in sorghum

evidence.title: A sorghum gigantea mutant attenuates florigen gene expression and delays flowering time

edges.mechanism: In LD, SbPRR37 upregulates SbCO expression at dawn (opposite to Arabidopsis). Differential SbCO expression between LD and SD requires PRR37. NOTE: this activation edge is paired with the PRR37-CO co-repressor action on SbCN8/12 (E025 below) — the net effect of PRR37 in LD is floral repression via the CO-Ehd1-FT axis.

evidence.title: Maturity2, a novel regulator of flowering time in Sorghum bicolor

edges.mechanism: SbFT10 binds Sb14-3-3 scaffold protein (florigen activation complex).

evidence.title: Photoperiod response and floral transition in sorghum
evidence.authors: Cai Y, Li Q, Liu X, Liu G, Wang L, Li Q, Ma T, Zhang Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbFT8 and SbFT10 directly interact with Sb14-3-3 but not with SbFD1.

edges.mechanism: Florigen activation complex (SbFT1-FD-14-3-3) activates SbSOC1 (SbMADS31) expression in the shoot apical meristem.

evidence.title: Photoperiod response and floral transition in sorghum
evidence.authors: Cai Y, Li Q, Liu X, Liu G, Wang L, Li Q, Ma T, Zhang Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: In Arabidopsis, FT together with SUPPRESSOR OF OVEREXPRESSION OF CONSTANS (SOC1), promotes expression of meristem identity gene LFY and AP1, leading to floral transition.

evidence.title: Genes and pathways determining flowering time variation in temperate-adapted sorghum
evidence.authors: Lemmon ZH, Stitzer MC, Jensen SE, Lovell JT, Siahkamari E, Morris GP, Washburn JD
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbMADS31 (Sobic.004G003434) encodes a SOC1 MADS-box transcription factor subfamily protein.
evidence.claim: SbMADS31 is the sorghum SOC1 ortholog

edges.mechanism: Florigen SbFT8 activates SbSOC1 (SbMADS31) in the meristem.

evidence.title: Photoperiod response and floral transition in sorghum
evidence.authors: Cai Y, Li Q, Liu X, Liu G, Wang L, Li Q, Ma T, Zhang Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: Three FLOWERING LOCUS T-like genes (SbFT1, SbFT8 and SbFT10)...serve as the ultimate mediators of photoperiod response and floral transition.
evidence.claim: SbFT8 activates SbSOC1 (conserved FT → SOC1 module)

edges.mechanism: Florigen SbFT10 activates SbSOC1 in the meristem (major florigen, directly repressed by Ghd7).

evidence.title: Sorghum SbGhd7 is a major regulator of floral transition and directly represses genes crucial for flowering activation
evidence.authors: Tadesse T, Lugassi N, Dragonas B, Shmuel YH, Gladman N, Yuan Y, Weers B, Mullet JE, Pasternak T, Leshkowitz D, Levy AA
evidence.evidence_sentence: FLOWERING LOCUS T 10 (SbFT10)...major florigen.
evidence.claim: SbFT10 is the major florigen driving floral transition (acts via SOC1/AP1 module)

edges.mechanism: Conserved SOC1 → LFY activation in the floral transition.

evidence.title: Photoperiod response and floral transition in sorghum
evidence.authors: Cai Y, Li Q, Liu X, Liu G, Wang L, Li Q, Ma T, Zhang Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: FT together with SUPPRESSOR OF OVEREXPRESSION OF CONSTANS (SOC1), promotes expression of meristem identity gene LFY and AP1.
evidence.claim: SOC1 activates LFY in sorghum meristem

edges.mechanism: SOC1 activates AP1 in the floral transition; SbMADS7 is the sorghum AP1/FUL clade MADS.

evidence.title: Genes and pathways determining flowering time variation in temperate-adapted sorghum
evidence.authors: Lemmon ZH, Stitzer MC, Jensen SE, Lovell JT, Siahkamari E, Morris GP, Washburn JD
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbMADS7 (Sobic.002G010100)...clusters with the AP1/FUL clade...act as regulators of floral initiation.
evidence.claim: SbMADS7 is sorghum AP1 ortholog, downstream of SOC1

edges.mechanism: LFY directly activates AP1 transcription; mutual reinforcement in floral meristem identity.

evidence.title: Photoperiod response and floral transition in sorghum
evidence.authors: Cai Y, Li Q, Liu X, Liu G, Wang L, Li Q, Ma T, Zhang Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: promotes expression of meristem identity gene LFY and AP1.
evidence.claim: LFY activates AP1 in sorghum floral transition

edges.mechanism: SbFT1 florigen promotes the vegetative-to-reproductive transition in the shoot apical meristem, reducing days to flowering.

evidence.title: Three FLOWERING LOCUS T-like genes function as potential florigens
evidence.authors: Wolabu TW, Zhang F, Niu L, Kalve S, Bhatnagar-Mathur P, Muszynski MG, Tadege M
evidence.evidence_sentence: three genes...activate flowering in transgenic Arabidopsis plants, suggesting florigenic potential.
evidence.claim: SbFT1 is a functional florigen accelerating flowering

edges.mechanism: SbFT8 florigen accelerates flowering transition.

evidence.title: Sorghum SbGhd7 is a major regulator of floral transition and directly represses genes crucial for flowering activation
evidence.authors: Tadesse T, Lugassi N, Dragonas B, Shmuel YH, Gladman N, Yuan Y, Weers B, Mullet JE, Pasternak T, Leshkowitz D, Levy AA
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbEhd1OE activated the promoters of three functional florigens (SbFT1, SbFT8 and SbFT10), dramatically accelerating flowering.

edges.mechanism: SbFT10 is the major florigen in sorghum (directly repressed by Ghd7); promotes flowering transition.

evidence.title: Sorghum SbGhd7 is a major regulator of floral transition and directly represses genes crucial for flowering activation
evidence.authors: Tadesse T, Lugassi N, Dragonas B, Shmuel YH, Gladman N, Yuan Y, Weers B, Mullet JE, Pasternak T, Leshkowitz D, Levy AA
evidence.evidence_sentence: repressing transcription of the major florigen FLOWERING LOCUS T 10 (SbFT10).

edges.mechanism: SbFT12 (Sb06g012260, FlrAvgD1, panicoid-specific PEBP) is a floral suppressor; loss-of-function confers photoperiod-insensitive early flowering. Reduction-of-function at CAAT-box deletion in 5'UTR drove temperate adaptation.

evidence.title: The Evolution of Photoperiod-Insensitive Flowering in Sorghum, A Genomic Model for Panicoid Grasses
evidence.authors: Cuevas HE, Zhou C, Tang H, Khadke PP, Das S, Lin YR, Ge Z, Clemente T, Upadhyaya HD, Hash CT, Paterson AH
evidence.evidence_sentence: wild-type Sb06g012260 is a floral suppressor.
evidence.claim: SbFT12 is a panicoid-specific floral suppressor delaying flowering

edges.mechanism: SbFT2 (Sobic.003G017200, ZCN14-like) expression in mature leaf tissue associates with earlier flowering (TWAS in temperate-adapted panel).

evidence.title: Genes and pathways determining flowering time variation in temperate-adapted sorghum
evidence.authors: Lemmon ZH, Stitzer MC, Jensen SE, Lovell JT, Siahkamari E, Morris GP, Washburn JD
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbFT2 (Sobic.003G017200)...expression of genes in mature leaf tissue was significantly associated with observed flowering time.

edges.mechanism: SbAP2 (Sobic.001G036800, rap2.7-ortholog) represses flowering; loss (via miR172) relieves repression.

evidence.title: Genes and pathways determining flowering time variation in temperate-adapted sorghum
evidence.authors: Lemmon ZH, Stitzer MC, Jensen SE, Lovell JT, Siahkamari E, Morris GP, Washburn JD
evidence.evidence_sentence: ortholog of the maize gene rap2.7 which acts as a negative regulator of flowering time.

edges.mechanism: SbSOC1 (SbMADS31) activates LFY/AP1 in the shoot apical meristem, triggering floral meristem identity.

evidence.title: Genes and pathways determining flowering time variation in temperate-adapted sorghum
evidence.authors: Lemmon ZH, Stitzer MC, Jensen SE, Lovell JT, Siahkamari E, Morris GP, Washburn JD
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbMADS31 (Sobic.004G003434) encodes a SOC1 MADS-box transcription factor subfamily protein.

edges.mechanism: SbAP1 (SbMADS7) commits the meristem to floral identity and triggers flowering.

evidence.title: Genes and pathways determining flowering time variation in temperate-adapted sorghum
evidence.authors: Lemmon ZH, Stitzer MC, Jensen SE, Lovell JT, Siahkamari E, Morris GP, Washburn JD
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbMADS7 (Sobic.002G010100)...act as regulators of floral initiation.

edges.mechanism: SbLFY, conserved meristem identity integrator, triggers floral transition.

evidence.title: Photoperiod response and floral transition in sorghum
evidence.authors: Cai Y, Li Q, Liu X, Liu G, Wang L, Li Q, Ma T, Zhang Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: promotes expression of meristem identity gene LFY and AP1, leading to floral transition.

edges.mechanism: Under inductive SD, early-morning peak of GA20/GA1 coincides with floral induction, hastening flowering. GA promotes flowering in grasses via DELLA destabilisation and FT activation.

evidence.title: Photoperiod Control of Gibberellin Levels and Flowering in Sorghum
evidence.authors: Foster KR, Morgan PW
evidence.evidence_sentence: under inductive 10-h photoperiods, the peak of GA20 and GA1 levels was shifted to an early morning peak, and flowering was hastened.
evidence.claim: Gibberellin peak timing correlates with accelerated flowering in sorghum SD

edges.mechanism: PhyB gates the timing of GA20 and GA1 peaks diurnally. In LD phyB-active plants, GA peak shifts to midday (non-inductive); in phyB-null plants, GA peaks shift to early morning (inductive for flowering).

evidence.title: Photoperiod Control of Gibberellin Levels and Flowering in Sorghum
evidence.authors: Foster KR, Morgan PW
evidence.evidence_sentence: The conversion of GA19→GA20 appears to be a critical control point regulated by phytochrome B.
evidence.claim: PhyB controls GA biosynthesis timing in sorghum

edges.mechanism: Long-day light evening phase coincides with SbPRR37 evening mRNA peak via PhyB; in SD the evening peak is eliminated because the phase occurs in darkness.

evidence.title: Coincident light and clock regulation of PRR37 controls photoperiodic flowering in sorghum
evidence.authors: Murphy RL, Klein RR, Morishige DT, Brady JA, Rooney WL, Miller FR, Dugas DV, Klein PE, Mullet JE
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
evidence.evidence_sentence: peaks of SbPRR37 mRNA abundance in the morning and in the evening...evening peak of SbPRR37 expression in SD does not occur due to darkness.
evidence.claim: LD photoperiod activates SbPRR37 evening peak

edges.mechanism: LD induces evening peak of SbGhd7 via PhyB; SD eliminates this peak (darkness).

evidence.title: Sorghum Phytochrome B Inhibits Flowering in Long Days by Activating Expression of SbPRR37 and SbGHD7
evidence.authors: Yang S, Weers BD, Morishige DT, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: evening peak of expression is attenuated in SD when this phase occurs in darkness.
evidence.claim: LD photoperiod activates SbGhd7 evening peak

edges.mechanism: Short-day treatment induces SbFT1 (and other florigens) in photoperiod-sensitive genotypes by relieving PRR37/Ghd7 repression.

evidence.title: Three FLOWERING LOCUS T-like genes function as potential florigens
evidence.authors: Wolabu TW, Zhang F, Niu L, Kalve S, Bhatnagar-Mathur P, Muszynski MG, Tadege M
evidence.evidence_sentence: induced by 1 wk SD treatment in photoperiod-sensitive genotypes.

edges.mechanism: SD induces SbFT8 (via de-repression by PRR37/Ghd7 decrease in SD).

evidence.title: Three FLOWERING LOCUS T-like genes function as potential florigens
evidence.authors: Wolabu TW, Zhang F, Niu L, Kalve S, Bhatnagar-Mathur P, Muszynski MG, Tadege M
evidence.evidence_sentence: induced by 1 wk SD treatment in photoperiod-sensitive genotypes.

edges.mechanism: SD induces SbFT10 (major florigen) by relieving Ghd7/PRR37 repression.

evidence.title: Three FLOWERING LOCUS T-like genes function as potential florigens
evidence.authors: Wolabu TW, Zhang F, Niu L, Kalve S, Bhatnagar-Mathur P, Muszynski MG, Tadege M
evidence.evidence_sentence: induced by 1 wk SD treatment in photoperiod-sensitive genotypes.

edges.mechanism: In LD when SbPRR37 is present, SbCO acts as a co-repressor of SbCN8 together with SbPRR37 (context-dependent sign flip from activator to repressor).

evidence.title: Maturity2, a novel regulator of flowering time in Sorghum bicolor
evidence.authors: Casto AL, McKinley BA, Yu KMJ, Rooney WL, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbPRR37 and SbCO are co-repressors of the expression of SbCN8 and SbCN12 in long days.
evidence.claim: SbCO co-represses SbCN8/SbFT8 with SbPRR37 in LD

edges.mechanism: SbEhd1 is a major floral activator whose overexpression dramatically accelerates flowering by upregulating all three functional florigens.

evidence.title: Sorghum SbGhd7 is a major regulator of floral transition
evidence.authors: Tadesse T, Lugassi N, Dragonas B, Shmuel YH, Gladman N, Yuan Y, Weers B, Mullet JE, Pasternak T, Leshkowitz D, Levy AA
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbEhd1OE activated the promoters of three functional florigens...dramatically accelerating flowering.
evidence.claim: SbEhd1 accelerates flowering via florigen activation

edges.mechanism: SbPRR37 is the key LD floral repressor; dominant Ma1 causes late LD flowering; loss of function (sbprr37) confers photoperiod insensitivity and earlier flowering.

evidence.title: Coincident light and clock regulation of PRR37 controls photoperiodic flowering in sorghum
evidence.authors: Murphy RL, Klein RR, Morishige DT, Brady JA, Rooney WL, Miller FR, Dugas DV, Klein PE, Mullet JE
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
evidence.evidence_sentence: Ma1, the major repressor of sorghum flowering in long days, was identified as the pseudoresponse regulator protein 37 (PRR37).

edges.mechanism: SbGhd7 is a strong LD floral repressor; overexpression completely prevents flowering. Acts in parallel with SbPRR37 (additive).

evidence.title: Sorghum SbGhd7 is a major regulator of floral transition
evidence.authors: Tadesse T, Lugassi N, Dragonas B, Shmuel YH, Gladman N, Yuan Y, Weers B, Mullet JE, Pasternak T, Leshkowitz D, Levy AA
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbGhd7 overexpression (OE) completely prevented sorghum flowering.

edges.mechanism: PhyB (Ma3) activates LD floral repressors PRR37+Ghd7; phyB null (ma3^R) shows ~60 days earlier LD flowering.

evidence.title: Sorghum Phytochrome B Inhibits Flowering in Long Days
evidence.authors: Yang S, Weers BD, Morishige DT, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: 58M phyB-1 vs 100M PHYB...Long Days (14h) 62 vs 126 days.
evidence.claim: PhyB represses flowering in LD (loss of PhyB = ~60 days earlier)

edges.mechanism: PhyC (Ma5) acts as a dominant LD flowering repressor; ma5 conferred strong flowering inhibition requires dominant allele.

evidence.title: Deleterious mutations predicted in sorghum Ma and Dw genes
evidence.authors: Thurber CS, Ma JM, Higgins RH, Brown PJ
evidence.evidence_sentence: Ma5 = SbPhyC...strong flowering inhibition (dominant form required).

edges.mechanism: Ma2 (SMYD-family methyltransferase) delays flowering in LD by enhancing PRR37+CO expression; ma2 (L141*) flowers ~100 days earlier than Ma2.

evidence.title: Maturity2, a novel regulator of flowering time in Sorghum bicolor
evidence.authors: Casto AL, McKinley BA, Yu KMJ, Rooney WL, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: 80M (ma2) flowers earlier than 100M (Ma2).

edges.mechanism: SbGI promotes flowering via CO-Ehd1-FT module; sbgi-ems1 shows ~30-day delay under LD and ~35-day delay under SD.

evidence.title: A sorghum gigantea mutant attenuates florigen gene expression and delays flowering time
evidence.authors: Abdul-Awal SM, Chen J, Xin Z, Harmon FG
evidence.evidence_sentence: sbgi-ems1 homozygous plants averaged 30 days more than sbgi-ems1 heterozygous and SbGI WT siblings.
evidence.claim: SbGI promotes flowering in both LD and SD

edges.mechanism: SbID1 establishes the capacity for flowering by activating both SbCO+florigens and SbPRR37+SbGhd7. Strong sbid1-2 mutant delays flowering ~170 days.

evidence.title: Sorghum bicolor INDETERMINATE1 is a conserved primary regulator of flowering
evidence.authors: Gladman N, Goodwin S, Chougule K, Richard McCombie W, Ware D
evidence.evidence_sentence: sbid1-2 (Q199*)...phenotype: ~170-day delay; near-complete flowering block.

edges.mechanism: SbCO activates SbEhd1+florigens, promoting flowering. Acts as activator in null-PRR37/Ghd7 backgrounds; in dominant Ma1Ma6 backgrounds CO contributes to LD co-repression.

evidence.title: CONSTANS is a photoperiod regulated activator of flowering in sorghum
evidence.authors: Yang S, Murphy RL, Morishige DT, Klein PE, Rooney WL, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: CO...functions as an activator of flowering in long and short days in sorghum genotypes using RILs with null versions of Sbprr37-1 and Sbghd7-1.

edges.mechanism: Circadian clock gates morning and evening SbPRR37 expression peaks (via SbGI and evening complex); even under continuous light the bimodal pattern persists.

evidence.title: Coincident light and clock regulation of PRR37 controls photoperiodic flowering in sorghum
evidence.authors: Murphy RL, Klein RR, Morishige DT, Brady JA, Rooney WL, Miller FR, Dugas DV, Klein PE, Mullet JE
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
evidence.evidence_sentence: circadian clock modulates SbPRR37 expression under...free-running conditions.

edges.mechanism: Circadian clock gates bimodal (morning + evening) SbGhd7 mRNA accumulation.

evidence.title: Sorghum Phytochrome B Inhibits Flowering in Long Days
evidence.authors: Yang S, Weers BD, Morishige DT, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbPRR37 and SbGHD7 RNA abundance peaks in the morning and in the evening...through coordinate regulation by light and output from the circadian clock.

edges.mechanism: SbCO expression is gated by the circadian clock; peaks regulated by SbPRR37, clock, and day length.

evidence.title: Sorghum Phytochrome B Inhibits Flowering in Long Days
evidence.authors: Yang S, Weers BD, Morishige DT, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbCO shows peaks of expression...regulated by SbPRR37, the circadian clock, and day length.

edges.mechanism: SbPRR37 expression is light-dependent: no expression observed under continuous darkness (DD).

evidence.title: Coincident light and clock regulation of PRR37 controls photoperiodic flowering in sorghum
evidence.authors: Murphy RL, Klein RR, Morishige DT, Brady JA, Rooney WL, Miller FR, Dugas DV, Klein PE, Mullet JE
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
evidence.evidence_sentence: In DD, neither peak of SbPRR37 expression was observed.
evidence.claim: Light is required for SbPRR37 expression

edges.mechanism: Rice Hd1 (sorghum SbHd1 = SbCO) forms heterotrimeric complex with NF-YB11 that directly binds Ghd7 promoter to activate Ghd7 transcription (rice evidence, conservation in sorghum implied).

evidence.title: Photoperiod response and floral transition in sorghum
evidence.authors: Cai Y, Li Q, Liu X, Liu G, Wang L, Li Q, Ma T, Zhang Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: Hd1...functions as an Ehd1 repressor through interaction with...Ghd7.
evidence.claim: Hd1-Ghd7 complex formed; cross-regulation in sorghum by conservation

edges.mechanism: In dominant PRR37+Ghd7 background (LD), SbCO co-represses SbCN12/SbFT10 with SbPRR37 — sign context-flip relative to null background where CO activates.

evidence.title: Maturity2, a novel regulator of flowering time in Sorghum bicolor
evidence.authors: Casto AL, McKinley BA, Yu KMJ, Rooney WL, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbPRR37 and SbCO are co-repressors of the expression of SbCN8 and SbCN12 in long days.

edges.mechanism: SbFT6 (Sb02g029725, near FlrAvgB1 QTL) associates with photoperiod response; likely a florigen-family activator of flowering.

evidence.title: The Evolution of Photoperiod-Insensitive Flowering in Sorghum
evidence.authors: Cuevas HE, Zhou C, Tang H, Khadke PP, Das S, Lin YR, Ge Z, Clemente T, Upadhyaya HD, Hash CT, Paterson AH
evidence.evidence_sentence: Sb02g029725 (SbFT6), a second FT family member mapping near the FlrAvgB1 QTL, showed highly significant photoperiod response association (P = 1.53×10⁻⁶).
evidence.claim: SbFT6 is associated with photoperiod flowering response

edges.mechanism: PhyB/PhyC mediate SbCO protein destabilisation in LD daylight hours, reducing CO activity (opposite of Arabidopsis); in SD this is released.

evidence.title: CONSTANS is a photoperiod regulated activator of flowering in sorghum
evidence.authors: Yang S, Murphy RL, Morishige DT, Klein PE, Rooney WL, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: Reduced PhyB/C-mediated degradation of SbCO in SD, relative to LD.
evidence.claim: PhyB promotes SbCO protein degradation in LD
